topic: Tidal M2 period — verify 2026-05-02 hedge patch closed c3 holes; check internal consistency vs monitoring_evidence
change_summary: Decider self-applied 2026-05-02 hedge (commit 3f34cec, ISS-1798/1812) to detail_verdict_text — replaced "are hardcoded static constants in monitor.py with no live data fetch at runtime" with "appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime (no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly)". detail_verdict_length grew 1038 → 1352 (+30%). Patch was driven by WIN-049.c4 cross-WIN finding. This c4 verifies whether the patch closed the c3 holes cleanly and checks for new inconsistencies introduced by the hedge.
our_argument_summary: WIN-045 argues the M2 tidal period (12.42h) is trivial — half a lunar day, derivable from any model that takes the 24.84h lunar circuit as input. The deeper, geometric argument is that the dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude produces a sharp single-spike tidal force field (9.24% of peak at 5,000 km offset, 0.56% at 14,000 km, 0.20% at the disc edge), contradicting the observed global two-bulge semidiurnal pattern. The post-hoc structural rebuttal in detail_verdict_text invokes Part 3.1b (trivial-transcription test) and the dome author's own definition of prospective prediction. Verdict: Self-Contradicted. Verdict holds on either argument alone; both together make this disposition very robust.

relevant_files: data/wins.json (WIN-045 entry, including code_analysis.monitoring_evidence as of 2026-04-25)
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 10 (verbatim source for the dome-author quoted phrase)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: ISS-1546 (closed 2026-04-25) records that a live monitor.py inspection on that date confirmed: domain 4 (M2 Tidal Period), m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2 (constant), m2_obs = 12.4206 (constant), no live data fetch. The audit artifact is preserved in code_analysis.monitoring_evidence on the WIN-045 entry. After this audit, the c4 review of WIN-049 (2026-05-02) flagged the cross-WIN posture mismatch, and the decider chose path (b) — propagate the WIN-049 hedge — rather than path (a) — cite the WIN-045 audit artifact and revert WIN-049 to the unhedged form. The chosen fix closed the cross-WIN-rhetorical-inconsistency hole but introduced a new internal-consistency issue specific to WIN-045 (see hole 1 below).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Period values (M2 = 12.42h) are static constants in monitor.py per the 2026-04-25 audit. No derivation from dome geometry is offered. Unchanged from cycle 3.
data_sources_used: M2 = 12.42h: NOAA standard oceanographic value; Doodson (1921)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Three known dome rescues unchanged from c3, all weak: (1) aetheric tidal mechanism (unfalsifiable, unpublished); (2) period-match alone is the prediction (circular under post-hoc); (3) observed tides are dynamical not equilibrium (true but the equilibrium forcing-field symmetry argument survives). New rescue available to advocate (see advocate_mode below): the dome could in principle invoke a non-Newtonian tidal forcing law that produces two bulges from a local moon, but no such law has been proposed.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: After 2026-05-02 hedge patch: detail_verdict_text now says "no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly". This contradicts WIN-045's own code_analysis.monitoring_evidence field, which states "Confirmed 2026-04-25 in current monitor.py domain 4 (M2 Tidal Period): m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2 (constant), m2_obs = 12.4206 (constant). No live data fetch." The audit was performed; the hedge mis-states the audit status for this specific WIN. WIN-046 and WIN-049 do not have monitoring_evidence populated, so the hedge is not internally inconsistent for them — it is specifically inconsistent for WIN-045.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed by direct monitor.py inspection 2026-04-25 (domain 4, m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2, m2_obs = 12.4206, no live fetch). Documented in WIN-045 code_analysis.monitoring_evidence field and ISS-1546 (closed 2026-04-25). c4 cycle: tag remains correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: M2 = 12.42h has been textbook physics since Doodson (1921). The dome contributes no new numerical value and offers no derivation from dome geometry. Unchanged from c3.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tidal constituent periods published since 1921. Tidal WINs entered the dome registry in V51.0 (April 2026) — a century after the value was tabulated. The trivial-transcription test (Part 3.1b) applies cleanly.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site starts from disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height_function H(r), or any aetheric parameter and arrives at 12.42 hours. The period is lunar_day / 2; the dome contributes no derivation step. Unchanged from c3.

holes_found.description: The 2026-05-02 hedge patch introduces an internal contradiction within WIN-045. The detail_verdict_text now says "appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime (no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly)". But the same WIN's code_analysis.monitoring_evidence field asserts "Confirmed 2026-04-25 in current monitor.py domain 4 (M2 Tidal Period): m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2 (constant), m2_obs = 12.4206 (constant). No live data fetch." An audit WAS performed for WIN-045 — ISS-1546 documents this and the artifact is preserved in the entry itself. A careful dome defender reading WIN-045 sees one field claim "no audit" and another claim "Confirmed 2026-04-25" and gets a free internal-inconsistency point. Note: WIN-046 and WIN-049 do NOT have monitoring_evidence populated, so the hedge is not self-contradictory for them — this is a WIN-045-specific issue that arises from path-(b) propagation when WIN-045 already had an audit artifact. The c4(WIN-049) review explicitly flagged path (a) — cite the artifact, revert WIN-049 — as the preferred fix; path (b) was chosen and applied uniformly without checking WIN-045's monitoring_evidence.
holes_found.recommendation: Two viable fixes. Preferred: open a small follow-up patch that for WIN-045 only revises the hedge clause to cite the audit, e.g., "both the predicted value (LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2) and observed value (12.4206 h) are static constants in monitor.py with no live data fetch at runtime (verified 2026-04-25, see ISS-1546)". This restores the unhedged form for WIN-045, eliminates the internal contradiction, and is more rhetorically forceful (we have the receipt, we should show it). Acceptable alternative: weaken the monitoring_evidence in WIN-045 to mirror the hedge — e.g., replace "Confirmed 2026-04-25" with "Per audit 2026-04-25 (ISS-1546), values appear to be static constants; no formal independent reproduction has been published". Less preferred because it understates the actual evidence we have. WIN-046 should ALSO get a monitoring_evidence field populated if a similar audit happened (S2 monitoring is presumably in the same monitor.py domain — bundle the audit work). WIN-049 should keep the hedge unless and until an IERS-source audit can be cited.

holes_found.description: The c3 review flagged the dome-author quote as a possible paraphrase. c4 verification: the quoted phrase "a number derived from the model, registered with a timestamp, and subsequently confirmed by independent data" IS verbatim from raw-text/02-wins.txt line 10. The c3 finding was incorrect; no action needed on this item. Logging here so a future spot-check does not re-raise it.
holes_found.recommendation: No prose action. Optionally annotate the WIN-045 entry or tracker that this quote has been verified verbatim against raw-text/02-wins.txt:10.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence still uses '~14,000 km' as the equator-distance from the sub-lunar point. c3 also flagged this. Quantitatively the order-of-magnitude argument is unchanged (the 14,000 km point gives 0.56% of peak; the disc edge at 20,015 km gives 0.20% — both ≪ 1%), so any reasonable choice of representative point yields the same qualitative conclusion. Not worth a standalone patch.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Bundle with future tidal-cluster prose work if any lands.

stronger_arguments.description: When citing the verbatim dome-author definition we should reverse the rhetorical move. The current detail_verdict_text uses the definition as a hurdle the dome cannot clear. We could go further: the dome's OWN monitor.py code (verified 2026-04-25, domain 4) registers the prediction and observation as static constants in the same file. Under the dome's own definition, the prediction is not "registered with a timestamp" before the data arrives — both the predicted value and the observed value are committed to git in the same commit, and have been since the tidal WINs were added in V51.0 (April 2026). This is not just "the test cannot apply" — it is the dome's own code demonstrating that the test has already failed by the dome's own standard. This is a stronger framing than "unverifiable" and is supported by the audit artifact we already have in monitoring_evidence.

The two-bulge falsification can be made even more concrete by naming a specific Antarctic tide gauge. McMurdo (Ross Sea) and Halley (Weddell Sea coast) both record M2 amplitudes in the ~30–50 cm range with semidiurnal phasing matching globe-tide theory. If the dome's local-moon model were correct, M2 amplitude would be enormous near the sub-lunar trajectory and effectively zero at high southern latitudes (where the dome's moon never passes overhead in the standard dome geometry). We could add one sentence to detail_evidence pointing to a specific Antarctic station amplitude — this converts the falsification from theoretical to verifiable in 2 minutes by anyone with internet access.
sources: British Antarctic Survey, Halley tide-gauge harmonic constituents (M2 ≈ 0.4–0.5 m)
McMurdo Station tidal records (M2 ≈ 0.5 m, semidiurnal phasing)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies that tidal periods are arithmetically related to astronomical input periods (lunar day, solar day, lunar nodal cycle). This is the foundational insight of Doodson harmonic analysis. Recognizing M2 = lunar_day/2 demonstrates basic competence in tidal taxonomy and is genuinely the right starting point for any tidal model (data/wins.json:WIN-045.detail_evidence). Unchanged from c3.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing taxonomy is not prediction. Any model that ingests a 24.84h lunar circuit produces M2 by division — the period is the input, not the output. The discriminating outputs of a tidal theory are amplitudes, phases, and spatial patterns, and on all three the dome model is silent or contradicted (data/wins.json:WIN-045.detail_evidence). M2 = 12.42h is the input; the spatial two-bulge pattern is the output that distinguishes a distant-moon model from a local-moon one. The dome gets the input right and the output wrong, which is the opposite of what "confirmed prediction" means. The 2026-04-25 monitor.py audit (ISS-1546; WIN-045 code_analysis.monitoring_evidence) further demonstrates that the dome's own implementation does not even attempt the discriminating amplitude/phase/spatial test — it simply asserts m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2 against m2_obs = 12.4206 as constants and prints "PASS".

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has accepted our methodological principles could argue as follows: "The reviewer's one-spike argument bakes in a Newtonian/general-relativistic tidal forcing law (∂g/∂r ∝ 1/r³) and applies it to the dome's 2,534 km moon. But the dome has not formally adopted the Newtonian tidal forcing function — that is the reviewer's assumption, not the dome's. If the dome publishes a non-Newtonian aetheric tidal coupling that produces two bulges from a local moon, the geometric argument evaporates. Under the reviewer's own internal-consistency principle, you cannot reject a model's prediction by inserting a forcing law the model has not adopted; you can only reject the model's prediction against the model's OWN forcing law. Until the dome publishes its tidal forcing function, the M2 disposition should be Not Demonstrated (mechanism unspecified) rather than Self-Contradicted (mechanism contradicted by data) — strictly weaker but more defensible." This defense exploits the gap between "the dome has not specified a tidal forcing function" and "the dome's tidal forcing function has been falsified."
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text making explicit that the geometric one-spike prediction ASSUMES Newtonian/inverse-cube tidal forcing. Frame the dome's escape route specifically: "The dome can escape this geometric prediction only by publishing a non-Newtonian aetheric tidal coupling that produces two roughly equal tidal bulges from a moon at 2,534 km altitude. No such coupling has been proposed, derived, or computed in any version of the model." This converts the advocate's strongest point — the unspecified mechanism — from a defense into an additional indictment (the dome owes a mechanism it has never offered). Severity-3 defense becomes severity-1 once the burden-of-proof is correctly assigned.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: After the 2026-05-02 hedge patch, the five tidal WINs use three distinct rhetorical postures in their post-hoc paragraphs: (a) WIN-045/046 — "appear to be static constants ... no independent code audit" (hedge); (b) WIN-049 — "appear to be static constants ... per the dashboard Source: IERS published label ... no independent code audit" (hedge plus IERS-label citation); (c) WIN-050/051 — "no monitor.py entry exists for this tidal constituent at all" (correct for monitoring='none'). Postures (b) and (c) are correct for their WINs. Posture (a) is correct for WIN-046 (no monitoring_evidence audit on file) but factually contradicts WIN-045's own code_analysis.monitoring_evidence (which documents a 2026-04-25 audit). The fix recommended above (revert WIN-045 only to a cited-audit form) restores cross-WIN consistency without forcing the same posture on WIN-046/049, where no audit artifact exists.

claims_checked: Globe gravitational variation across Earth diameter
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct python calculation. h=2534 km. At d=5000: distance=5605.5, ratio=9.24% (review: 9%). At d=14000: distance=14227.5, ratio=0.56% (review: 0.6%). At d=20015 (disc edge): distance=20174.8, ratio=0.20%. Globe near-side=378029, far-side=390771: gravitational ratio diff = 6.42% (review: 6.6%); tidal-cube diff = 9.47% (review: ~10%). M2: 24.84/2 = 12.420 h vs observed 12.4206 h — match to 4 decimals. Far-edge distance = 20174.8 km, sub-lunar distance = 2534 km, ratio = 7.96x (review: 8x).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 verifies that the 2026-05-02 hedge patch (commit 3f34cec; ISS-1798, ISS-1812) closed the cross-WIN-rhetorical-mismatch hole flagged by WIN-049.c4. WIN-045 and WIN-046 detail_verdict_text now share posture with WIN-049: "appear to be static constants ... no independent code audit". WIN-050/051 retain the correct "none"-case prose ("no monitor.py entry exists at all"). Tag-versus-prose self-consistency holds for WIN-046, WIN-049, WIN-050, WIN-051.

The c3 finding-1 recommendation (path (a): cite audit artifact and revert hedge) was the better path for WIN-045 specifically because ISS-1546 (closed 2026-04-25) preserved a direct monitor.py audit in code_analysis.monitoring_evidence. The decider chose path (b) (propagate hedge uniformly) which is the right move when you don't know which WINs have audits, but creates a NEW internal contradiction for WIN-045: the hedge clause says "no independent audit" while the same WIN's monitoring_evidence says "Confirmed 2026-04-25". This is captured as moderate hole 1 above.

The verdict (Self-Contradicted) is rock-solid on either the geometric or the post-hoc argument. None of the c4 holes threaten the verdict; they all bear on rhetorical precision and internal consistency.

Advocate-mode pass surfaces a defense rated 3/5 — the dome can in principle escape the geometric argument by publishing a non-Newtonian tidal forcing law, and we currently do not preempt this move. Adding one sentence that names this escape and notes it has not been taken converts a defensible move into an additional indictment.

Quantitative verification: all five numerical claims in detail_evidence verified to within rounding (9.24% vs review 9%, 0.56% vs 0.6%, 6.42% vs 6.6%, 9.47% vs ~10%, 7.96x vs 8x; M2 = 12.420 vs 12.4206 to 4 decimals).

Confidence 0.93, up slightly from c3's 0.92: the cross-WIN hedge propagation was a real improvement and the verdict has now survived four cycles of attack including this c4 audit. The remaining moderate hole is a rhetorical-precision issue, not a verdict-destabilizing issue.
